campbell.title: Campbell City Council — April 21, 2026
campbell.summary: Campbell's city council met to discuss and vote on changes to business license tax rates for the upcoming fiscal year. Finance department staff presented the proposed updates to the city's business tax rules, and council members debated the changes before making a final decision.
keyTopics: Business license tax rates - annual update and changes
City council review and voting on the proposed tax rate changes
Finance department presentation of the ordinance details
County emergency preparedness office involvement

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026 to get community input on a proposed new rule or policy. After hearing from residents, the council moved forward by introducing the ordinance and skipping the usual first reading process, with plans to vote on final approval at their next meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held to gather resident feedback on new ordinance
First reading of ordinance waived to speed up approval process
Ordinance scheduled for final vote at next regular council meeting
Community input considered before moving to adoption phase

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 21, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on April 21, 2026, to approve routine meeting minutes and review the city's finances from February. The council also authorized the city manager to sign several contracts for construction work and professional services, though the full details of these agreements were cut off in the agenda.
keyTopics: Approved previous meeting minutes from April 7
Reviewed city's February 2026 financial and investment report
Authorized construction contracts with QLM, Inc. and other firms
Approved multi-year professional services agreement

san-jose.title: San José City Council — May 5, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council met to approve routine meeting minutes and discussed several projects affecting the city. Main items included replacing an old elevator at a terminal building, hiring companies to clean up homeless encampments, updating a digital billboard agreement with Clear Channel, and approving a firefighter memorial event in District 7.
keyTopics: Terminal Elevator Replacement Project - infrastructure upgrade
Encampment Cleanup Services - new contract to address homeless sites
Digital Signage Agreement - changes to large format ads on city property
Light the Night for Fallen Firefighters - memorial event approval and funding

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 30, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council met to approve meeting minutes and hear from residents during public comment. The main focus was reviewing and recommending which neighborhood grant requests should receive funding for the 2026-27 budget year, along with discussing attendance at neighborhood meetings.
keyTopics: Neighborhood grant funding decisions for 2026-27
Public comment period for residents
Neighborhood meeting attendance review
Meeting minutes approval

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 28, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held a special evening meeting on April 28, 2026, to interview candidates for open positions on city boards and commissions. Residents were able to watch and participate online, and those in person had a chance to provide public comment before the meeting ended.
keyTopics: Board and commission candidate interviews
In-person public comment opportunity
Online participation option for residents
Study session format for evaluation and discussion

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — May 5, 2026
cupertino.summary: The city council held a private negotiation meeting about buying or selling a property at 10480 Finch Avenue between the City of Cupertino and the local school district. The city manager and interim city attorney discussed the price and payment terms for this real estate deal. The council also reviewed the Silicon Valley Hopper program and discussed plans for getting future grant funding.
keyTopics: Property deal negotiations at 10480 Finch Avenue with the school district
Price and payment terms being worked out for the property transaction
Silicon Valley Hopper program overview and future grant funding strategy

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 21, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The City Council met to discuss two main items: allowing large promotional signs to be displayed on buildings around Levi's Stadium during the 2026 FIFA World Cup, and approving a new employment agreement with the city's firefighters union that will run through 2029. One agenda item was withdrawn by staff before the meeting.
keyTopics: Temporary supergraphic signs permitted near Levi's Stadium for World Cup 2026 events
New firefighter contract agreement covering 2026-2029
Staff withdrew one item from the agenda

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — May 5, 2026
milpitas.summary: Milpitas city leaders met to plan the city's spending for the next several years. They discussed how much money the city will have to spend in 2026-27, approved a plan for new parks and trails in the Innovation District area, and made updates to how the City Council runs its meetings.
keyTopics: Five-year capital improvement plan (2027-2031) - what big projects the city will build or fix
2026-27 operating budget - how much money the city will spend on day-to-day operations
Innovation District Parks and Trails Master Plan - new recreational spaces coming to that area
Updates to City Council procedures - changes to how meetings are conducted

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 29, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met to discuss routine business items and a major development proposal for Old Trace Road. The main focus was a request to subdivide a vacant lot into 9 residential lots that would allow construction of 9 new single-family homes and 7 junior accessory dwelling units (smaller secondary homes on the properties).
keyTopics: Subdivision of 1.02-acre lot on Old Trace Road into 9 residential parcels
Construction of 9 new single-family homes plus 7 junior accessory dwelling units
Creation of a new private street to access the development
Election of new City Council chair and vice chair
Development is exempt from full environmental review under state law
